title: The Anglerfish and the Hairy Drawing
summary: Hatty the Mouse and her friend Izzy the Kiddo spend a whimsical day drawing portraits that span from the sunny poolside to the deep, dark sea where anglerfish glow. Joined by Christopher Robin, Pooh-bear, and a cast of artistic friends like Piglet and Mischief, the group explores a gallery of "hairy drawings" and ever-changing sketches. Their art transforms from lantern fish into swings and walks with mom. As the stars emerge, the friends admire each other's work one last time before waving goodbye and returning home for their well-earned naps.

highlightQuote.text: No, it's not a lantern fish, I think it's an anglerfish. No, maybe... no, it's not a purple one, it's a blue one. No, no, it's a junk fish, I think.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Dad, can you please get out my drawing pen so I can draw something?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I thought we were going to do stars and stories first.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, first I'm going to draw a little portrait. First I want to draw...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let me just set the timer.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, could you please get out my pen?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, it's tricky, isn't it? There you go. Who are you going to make a portrait of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Me. In me, watching me... head... hair...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's my toes...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: One piece of hair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: One piece of hair, I see. I like that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's long. Even longer than my hair that I have now. See? That's me!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's you with your long hair. I love it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: See, that's me sliding at the pool.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! It's a portrait of Izzy. I love it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Swimming in the pool, doing a somersault.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's me!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm gliding and doing a somersault.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I love that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now I'm going even deeper in the dark to where the anglerfish live.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. That's so deep.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I've got my diving suit on with stripes... stripe, stripe, stripe.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see the stripes. I like how you're drawing stripes on your diving suit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then I just have... hair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, there's a little bit of hair, yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There. There! That's me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's Izzy, yes. Are you ready for stars and stories?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll turn off the big light. And now it's stars and stories time. You ready?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Please about Hatty.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Always about Hatty.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now I'll try another drawing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First I draw a cage. This is my cage. Dad, do you see my cage?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, it's hard to see because it's dark, but I do see that you're drawing, yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do some more hair on some more...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you want more light? For drawing? Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is not a hairy drawing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I'm going to make it a hairy drawing. Hairy drawing. It's a little hairy. Then I draw a really big hairy drawing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Big hairy drawing!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, it's a big hairy drawing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a big hairy drawing. But it's not very big. A main drawing. That's a small one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I made a big one!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A very big one. Oh, yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I remember what else to draw... circle... oval... draw a head...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What did you draw?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, see, I draw... I drew Avery.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You drew Avery? Show me.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here's Avery. See? She's wearing her different clothes of Avery. See? Big clothes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let me erase this drawing. This drawing isn't so good. Let me draw a different one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First I start with... hair... one hand... and another hand.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This doesn't look like Avery.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I like drawing with you, but we're going to run out of time for stars and stories. Do you want to draw one more drawing and then do one short story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A big circle... and add this. What am I drawing now?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that could be a person. Are those legs?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, maybe it's a jellyfish.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, it could be if you want it to. But see, I'm drawing spines. Maybe it could be an anglerfish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Maybe it could be an anglerfish. I love that idea. So it just needs a little lantern on the front.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm going to put it on the back. Oh, here's the... light... little bit of hair... and just one tiny... sorry.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you even tell the story when the light is off?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Okay, let's put away the drawing table.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not putting away the drawing table, busy!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you want to just tell the story right now like this?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Should I turn out the light or no?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, because I need light to see what I draw.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well we're going to do a quick story then. Once upon a time, Hatty the mouse and Izzy the kiddo were drawing together, and they were both drawing pictures of all kinds of things. But all of a sudden, Izzy was drawing something special. It was a beautiful, complicated drawing with lots of different parts. And Hatty the mouse said, 'What's that?' And Izzy said, 'It's a lantern fish.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hatty said, 'What's a lantern fish?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's not a lantern fish, I think it's an anglerfish. No, maybe... no, it's not a purple one, it's a blue one. No, no, it's a junk fish, I think.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And Hatty said, 'Wow, those all sound like very interesting fish. You could draw all of them if you want to.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'll draw a swimming in the... over the stars. There. There! There we go. That's my whole little thing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Christopher Robin came over to visit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Christopher Robin, look what I drew!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's such a beautiful drawing. What is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is a portrait of myself going for a walk. Oh, and it looks like an anglerfish but it's not, it's me on the swing going for a walk. Looks like it's me and my mom, but it's just me going for a walk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's you, Izzy, going for a walk. I see. That's a very interesting picture. Thanks for sharing it with me.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Christopher Robin... oh...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Christopher Robin said, 'Oh, and Hatty the mouse, what have you drawn?' And what did Hatty say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was drawing with my friend Mischief and my friend Silly-Silly Pooh-bear and Piglet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you've all been drawing together. That sounds very nice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, I need to draw more things. Can you keep telling the story while I try to get out this pen? Dad, can you please get out this pen for me? So I can draw more things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, and it's almost time for the story to be over, so let's... let's find a way to end it. And so Christopher Robin said, 'Wow, Izzy and Hatty, those are beautiful drawings. I'd like to show you my drawing someday. Maybe we can all draw together. But now it's time for me to go home as it's time for me to take a nap. Bye-bye!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hatty the mouse said, 'It's time for my nap too. Goodbye everyone.' And Izzy said...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, I have to go home too, because it's time for me to go to sleep too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And everyone said goodbye and they took their drawings with them and they all went home. And that was the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There. There!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And now it's time to turn off the stars...

characters.name: Hatty the Mouse
characters.description: A small mouse who loves to draw and is friends with Izzy the Kiddo.

characters.name: Izzy the Kiddo
characters.description: Once a quiet dreamer sketching from a recovery bed, this brave explorer now leads her companions through Hattie’s workshop and the lightless abyss, guided by a beacon of special light. Clad in a rugged driving suit alongside Bluey and Fincat’s Mouse, she wields heavy hammers and a magic wand with logistical precision inherited from her Nana. From trading plastic bricks for rare mushrooms to orchestrating neighborhood cleanups, she balances specialized gear with a playful spirit. Choosing the spark of a game over the gravity of a traditional quest, she ensures her radiant creativity and guiding light remain her most powerful tools for navigating the unknown.

characters.name: Avery
characters.description: Now christened [Name], this diminutive mole is the newest addition to Izzy’s family and Hattie’s velvety baby sister. A tiny voyager often depicted in portraits wearing oversized clothes, she is a slumbering resident aboard the ship, finding bliss in milk-filled dreams within her cozy "house" upon the storyteller’s knee. To protect [Name]’s fragile rest, the crew maintains a state of hushed reverence, ensuring the vessel remains a quiet sanctuary for its smallest, most loyal passenger.

characters.name: Christopher Robin
characters.description: The master of the house at the final destination is a gentle boy and companion to Tigger, recently returned from a fishing trip to serve as the catalyst for the pilgrimage to the Wizard’s castle. Navigating the halls in a small car, he wanders the gallery of existence with a quiet, unsettling grace, pausing as a helpful friend to assist in inflating balloons. A reverent guest in his own domain, he visits Piglet—the foundational architect of a looming "big bad"—before setting the gears of the quest in motion and retiring home for a nap.

characters.name: Winnie-the-Pooh
characters.description: A bear friend also known as Pooh-bear who joins the drawing session.

characters.name: Piglet
characters.description: A diminutive and loyal companion, this small, thoughtful creature balances high-spirited play with Christopher Robin against a dedicated role in the group’s collaborative drawing sessions. Ever-attentive and earnest, he now channels his quiet focus into a heartfelt quest: discovering the perfect birthday gift for a cherished friend.

characters.name: Mischief
characters.description: Proprietor of the analog-scented tape store on the corner of Poplar and Popcornioni, this avian known as Velociraptorning is Hattie’s loyal companion and the devoted mother of Mistress Mouse. Whether aiding Oscar on his adventures from their shared burrow or tending her wall of magnetic spools, she remains a soul of simple pleasures—sketching in sun-drenched parks, sharing meals of corn, and admiring her daughter’s new hat with a prideful, artistic eye.

characters.name: Silly-Silly
characters.description: A playful friend mentioned as part of the gathering of artistic friends.

places.name: The Pool
places.description: A place where Izzy swims, slides, and performs somersaults.

places.name: The Deep Sea
places.description: A dark place inhabited by anglerfish where Izzy dives in her striped suit.

places.name: Home
places.description: A sanctuary of restorative rest where weary creators retire after the day’s long labors of drawing, this haven is destined to become the permanent hearth for all friends once the blight of Frasgle has been purged and the land is clean again.
